Itemized  ·  Compare  ·  Brigham and Women's Hospital vs Lahey Hospital & Medical Center

Brigham and Women's Hospital vs Lahey Hospital & Medical Center.

Side-by-side prices for 28 procedures both hospitals publish, plus CMS quality ratings and metro context. Pulled from each hospital's federally-mandated price transparency file. Lahey Hospital & Medical Center is cheaper on more procedures (16 vs 11).

Brigham and Women's Hospital
Boston, MA
Mass General Brigham
5/5 CMS
Cheaper on 11 of 28 procedures
vs.
Lahey Hospital & Medical Center
Burlington, MA
Beth Israel Lahey Health
5/5 CMS
Cheaper on 16 of 28 procedures

Head-to-head, by procedure.

Procedure Brigham Lahey Cheaper
Brain MRI without contrast $3,858 $2,945 Lahey ↓
Brain MRI with and without contrast $4,861 $4,115 Lahey ↓
Lumbar spine MRI without contrast $3,281 $2,945 Lahey ↓

CT abdomen and pelvis with contrast $2,130 $1,991 Lahey ↓

Mammogram, screening $506 $728 Brigham ↓

Chest X-ray, single view $281 $206 Lahey ↓
Comprehensive metabolic panel $134 $127 Lahey ↓

TSH (thyroid stimulating hormone) $150 $113 Lahey ↓
Hemoglobin A1c $122 $75 Lahey ↓
Inguinal hernia repair, open $2,078 — tie

Colonoscopy with polyp removal $1,964 $4,044 Brigham ↓
Upper endoscopy (EGD) with biopsy $2,769 $2,825 Brigham ↓

Prostate biopsy $513 $2,930 Brigham ↓
Carotid duplex ultrasound $1,909 $1,009 Lahey ↓
Psychotherapy, 45 minutes $237 $366 Brigham ↓

Patient experience.

CMS HCAHPS patient survey, period ending 03/31/2025. Bold = higher score.

Measure Brigham Lahey
HCAHPS overall star rating 4/5 3/5
Would definitely recommend 83% 72%
Hospital rating 9 or 10 of 10 77% 70%
Nurses always communicated well 83% 80%
Doctors always communicated well 82% 81%
Given clear info about recovery 89% 87%
Room and bathroom always clean 68% 62%
Staff always explained meds 59% 61%
Quiet at night, always 48% 50%

Add your insurance.

Cash-pay is one number. With your insurance plan, the actual price differs. Pick your insurer in the comparison tool to see plan-specific rates at both hospitals.

Open comparison →

How to read this comparison.

The cash-pay price is what an uninsured patient would be charged at each hospital. It's the cleanest apples-to-apples comparison because it doesn't depend on your insurance plan.

The CMS rating is the federal quality composite, built from ~50 measures spanning safety, mortality, readmission, patient experience, and timeliness. A 5-star hospital may not be the best at every procedure, and a 3-star hospital can have a strong specific service line. Treat the rating as one input, not the answer.

For your specific insurance plan, prices can shift dramatically. Some hospitals negotiate steep discounts with one insurer and not another. Always check the plan-specific rate before you book.

Data sources: CMS Hospital Price Transparency rule (45 CFR 180.50). CMS Hospital Care Compare (xubh-q36u). CMS HCAHPS patient survey (dgck-syfz). Last refresh: 2026-04-27.
A consumer reading of CMS-mandated MRF data. Not medical or financial advice. Itemized · itemized.health
